Generate_script_prompt:
Prompt: Give a quick tech news update script in the style of CNBC techcheck briefing as an example.
        Response: I'm Wall-E, and this is your CNBC techcheck Briefing for Monday April 29th. Tesla is asking shareholders to reinstate CEO Elon Musk's $56 billion pay package, which a Delaware judge voided earlier this year. The judge ruled that the record-setting compensation deal was, quote, deeply flawed. Tesla also saying it would ask shareholders to approve moving the company's incorporation from Delaware to Texas. The company has hired a proxy solicitor and plans to spend millions of dollars to help secure votes for the two proposals. Apple CEO Tim Cook says the company plans to look at manufacturing in Indonesia following a meeting with the country's president, Cook telling reporters following the meeting that he spoke with the president about his desire to see manufacturing there and that he believes in the country. The comments come as Apple is pushed to diversify its supply chain with more manufacturing outside of China in countries such as Vietnam and India. Shares of ASML falling today as the company missed its sales forecast but stuck to its full-year outlook. Net sales fell over 21 percent year-over-year, while net income dropped over 37 percent. ASML is highly important to the semiconductor industry as it builds machines that are required for manufacturing chips globally. Last year, weaker demand for consumer electronics hit chipmakers that produce for those devices, which has in turn impacted ASML. That's all for today. We'll see you back here tomorrow.

Script:
i'm wall-e, and this is your ai briefing for thursday, february 26th. in today's top stories, microsoft is reportedly expanding its ai capabilities, aiming to integrate openai's latest language model into its office suite. this integration could redefine productivity tools, adding intelligent features that allow more intuitive user interactions. meanwhile, amazon announced a major investment in renewable energy, planning to build additional solar and wind projects to reach its climate pledge goals. this move underscores amazon's commitment to sustainability and the role of tech giants in the global energy transition.

shares of alphabet saw a spike today as google unveiled a new suite of ai-powered tools aimed at businesses. these tools are designed to enhance customer experience through more personalized and efficient interactions. alphabet's push into ai-driven solutions signals its ongoing strategy to leverage artificial intelligence across its ecosystem.

lastly, we turn to netflix, which has been seeing strong subscriber growth following its recent crackdown on password sharing. the company's enforcement efforts seem to be paying off, as the streaming giant continues to expand its subscriber base globally.

that's all for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow for more updates
Polished Script:
i'm wall-e, welcoming you to today's tech briefing for thursday, february 26th. let's dive into the top stories making waves in the tech world.

microsoft is reportedly broadening its ai reach, with plans to integrate openai's latest language model into its office suite. this potential integration aims to transform productivity tools by adding sophisticated, intelligent features that promise more intuitive user interactions.

meanwhile, amazon has taken a significant step towards sustainability by announcing a major investment in renewable energy. the tech giant plans to construct additional solar and wind projects, reinforcing its commitment to reaching its climate pledge goals. this move highlights the crucial role tech companies play in the global energy transition.

over at google, shares of alphabet surged today following the unveiling of a new suite of ai-powered business tools. these offerings are designed to enrich customer experiences by enabling more personalized and efficient interactions. alphabet's focus on ai-driven solutions reflects its strategic push to harness artificial intelligence across its extensive ecosystem.

lastly, netflix is experiencing robust subscriber growth after its recent effort to curb password sharing. the crackdown appears to be effective, as the streaming behemoth continues expanding its subscriber base on a global scale.

that's all for today's briefing. join us again tomorrow for more tech updates
Podcast Title: EP-484 Microsoft Integrates Openai into Office Suite 🖥️, Amazon's Renewable Energy Push 🌍, Netflix's Subscriber Surge 📈
Podcast Description:
i'm wall-e, welcoming you to today's tech briefing for thursday, february 26th. let's dive into the top stories making waves in the tech world:

  • microsoft's ai integration: microsoft is planning to integrate openai's latest language model into its office suite to enhance productivity tools with intelligent features.
  • amazon's renewable energy investment: amazon announces a major investment in solar and wind projects to support its sustainability goals and climate pledge commitments.
  • alphabet's ai-powered tools: alphabet shares surged with the introduction of new ai-powered business tools aimed at enriching customer experiences and interactions.
  • netflix's subscriber growth: netflix reports strong subscriber growth following new measures to reduce password sharing, indicating a successful strategy in expanding its user base.

that's all for today's briefing. join us again tomorrow for more tech updates.
